title: Intel macからsilicon MBPに変更したため、intel mac用のアプリのエラーが多発している。徹底的に調べてエラーが出ないようにして
summary_text: 1. Top risks/regressions - `brew` is still architecture-sensitive to the parent shell. In this session, `zsh -lic 'brew config'` reports `macOS: 26.4-x86_64`, while `arch -arm64 ... brew config` reports `macOS: 26.4-arm64`. That means Rosetta-launched shells can silently keep Homebrew in x86 mode even with `/opt/homebrew`. - Intel fallback paths are still present in wrappers. [`/Users/masayuki/bin/node`](/Users/masayuki/bin/node), [`/Users/masayuki/bin/npm`](/Users/masayuki/bin/npm), and [`/Users/masayuki/bin/npx`](/Users/masayuki/bin/npx) include `.nvm` fallback candidates; the `.nvm` node binary is x86_64. - `codex` wrapper is pinned to `.nvm` paths and has an explicit x64 fallback in [`/Users/masayuki/bin/codex`](/Users/masayuki/bin/codex). This can reintroduce Intel execution even after other cleanup. - Interactive shell behavior is not deterministic: `npm` is currently a shell function (from shell startup), not just a binary, so behavior can diverge across contexts. 2. Assumptions that need verification - Terminal host apps are not launched with “Open using Rosetta” (this is currently likely false for at least one execution path). - “Normal” login shell path resolution (`command -v brew`) stays on `/Users/masayuki/bin/brew` and keeps `/opt/homebrew` primary. - No critical LaunchAgent/cron job invokes `/usr/local/bin/brew` or `.nvm/.../node` directly. - `.nvm` remains non-authoritative for runtime-critical commands, or is migrated to arm64 if still required. - Native `brew` installs/taps were not polluted by x86 invocations during transition. 3. Stop conditions - Stop if `brew config` still shows `x86_64` in the user’s standard login shell after any PATH/wrapper change. At that point, fix the launcher architecture first, not config churn. - Stop before any deletion of `/usr/local`, `.nvm`, or app bundles unless explicitly approved (high blast radius). - Stop if wrapper edits break command availability for `node/npm/npx/codex` in a clean login shell. - Stop if any background service health checks regress after path/arch changes. 4. Reasons to avoid changing shell config or wrappers broadly - [`/Users/masayuki/.zshrc`](/Users/masayuki/.zshrc) is large, heavily customized, and includes secrets/keychain exports plus many aliases/functions; broad edits carry high regression risk. - The safer path is minimal, surgical changes to explicit wrappers (especially `brew` and `codex`) and architecture validation, rather than global PATH refactors in shell startup files. - Wrapper fallback behavior should be tightened carefully; removing Intel fallbacks too aggressively can strand commands if an arm64 binary path is missing.
